factors.summary: Capital expenditures are increasing.

evidence.text: Capital expenditures were $2.3 billion and $2.0 billion in the nine months ended September 30, 2016 and 2015, respectively. Capital expenditures for the nine months ended September 30, 2016 were primarily attributable to the purchase of aircraft, facility and fleet-related costs.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Increasing capital expenditures indicate investment in growth opportunities.

factors.summary: Shareholder returns are increasing.

evidence.text: In the three and nine months ended September 30, 2016, UAL repurchased 5 million and 48 million shares of UAL common stock in open market transactions, respectively, for $255 million and $2.4 billion, respectively.

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: Significant increase in share repurchases shows strong returns to shareholders.

factors.summary: Pricing power is decreasing as evidenced by declining PRASM and yield.

evidence.text: Consolidated PRASM decreased 5.8% compared to the third quarter of 2015. The decline in PRASM was driven by factors including a strong U.S. dollar, lower surcharges, reductions from energy-related corporate travel, and declining yields.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Declining PRASM and yield suggest weakened pricing power.

factors.summary: Organic revenue growth is decelerating as net income and operating income have decreased year-over-year.

evidence.text: Net income was $965 million in the third quarter of 2016 as compared to $4.8 billion in the third quarter of 2015. Operating income was $1.6 billion for the third quarter of 2016, as compared to $1.9 billion for the third quarter of 2015, a $0.3 billion decrease year-over-year.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Decrease in net and operating income indicates growth deceleration.

factors.summary: Unit volumes are growing as passenger numbers and RPMs increased year-over-year.

evidence.text: Passengers increased 3.2% year-over-year, and RPMs increased 1.8% year-over-year.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Growth in passengers and RPMs shows increasing demand.

factors.summary: Gross and operating margins are compressing due to declining revenue and increasing costs.

evidence.text: Operating income decreased 14.5% year-over-year. Nonoperating expense increased 61.1% year-over-year.
evidence.subsection: Third Quarter 2016 Compared to Third Quarter 2015

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Decreasing margins due to lower revenue and higher costs.

factors.summary: Productivity is improving as evidenced by lower fuel costs and higher operational efficiency.

evidence.text: Aircraft fuel expense decreased $331 million, or 17.1%, year-over-year primarily due to a 18.7% decrease in the average price per gallon of aircraft fuel in the third quarter of 2016 compared to the year-ago period. The Company achieved best third-quarter and best year-to-date on-time performance in its history.

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: Improved productivity with lower fuel costs and better efficiency.

factors.summary: Management expects capacity growth and value capture through operational improvements.

evidence.text: The Company expects full-year 2016 consolidated capacity to increase between 1.2% and 1.4% year-over-year. The Company expects to drive $3.1 billion of incremental value by 2018.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Positive outlook with expected capacity growth and value creation.

factors.summary: Near-term risks include fuel price volatility and ongoing labor negotiations.

evidence.text: The price of jet fuel remains volatile. [...] Increases in the pay and benefits resulting from new collective bargaining agreements could have a material financial impact on the Company.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Evidence highlights significant risks from fuel price volatility and labor negotiations.

factors.summary: The company's long-term strategy includes capacity growth, product differentiation, and operational efficiency.

evidence.text: The Company expects full-year 2016 consolidated capacity to increase between 1.2% and 1.4% year-over-year. The Company expects to drive $3.1 billion of incremental value by 2018. United anticipates to capture this value through benefits from improved operations by focusing on re-attracting premium customers, reducing cost of irregular operations, reducing re-accommodations and improving schedule quality.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Evidence shows clear strategic initiatives with expected capacity growth and value creation.

factors.summary: Key sensitivities include fuel prices and foreign exchange rates.

evidence.text: The price of jet fuel remains volatile. [...] Foreign currency losses were approximately $4 million and $81 million in the third quarters of 2016 and 2015, respectively.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Evidence points to significant risks from fuel price volatility and foreign exchange losses.

factors.summary: New products and initiatives are expected to improve results.

evidence.text: The Company will introduce new products to its industry-leading existing portfolio of products such as our recently announced Polaris business class seats and enhance revenue through various MileagePlus program initiatives.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Evidence indicates new products and initiatives expected to improve results.

factors.summary: Capacity is increasing slightly, load factor is stable, and revenue per available seat mile is declining.

evidence.text: Consolidated capacity increased 2.0% during the third quarter of 2016 as compared to the third quarter of 2015. The Company's load factor for the third quarter of 2016 was 85.5%. Consolidated PRASM decreased 5.8% compared to the third quarter of 2015.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Evidence shows declining revenue per available seat mile despite capacity growth.

factors.summary: The demand mix is shifting unfavorably with lower yields and PRASM.

evidence.text: Consolidated PRASM decreased 5.8% compared to the third quarter of 2015. The decline in PRASM was driven by factors including a strong U.S. dollar, lower surcharges, reductions from energy-related corporate travel, and declining yields.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Evidence indicates unfavorable demand mix with declining yields and PRASM.

factors.summary: The company is differentiating through new products and customer experience initiatives.

evidence.text: The Company will introduce new products to its industry-leading existing portfolio of products such as our recently announced Polaris business class seats and enhance revenue through various MileagePlus program initiatives.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Evidence supports differentiation through new products and customer experience initiatives.

factors.summary: New products are being introduced, which is expected to improve results.

evidence.text: The Company will introduce new products to its industry-leading existing portfolio of products such as our recently announced Polaris business class seats and enhance revenue through various MileagePlus program initiatives.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Evidence highlights new product introductions expected to improve results.

factors.summary: Demand appears to be mixed, with evidence suggesting acceleration in some areas and stability with slight growth in others. While there are signs of increased operational performance and employee incentives indicating higher demand, there is also a decline in pricing power as reflected by the decrease in PRASM.

evidence.text: Salaries and related costs increased $418 million or 5.7%, in the first nine months of 2016 as compared to the year-ago period primarily due to higher pay rates and benefit expenses driven by new and extended collective bargaining agreements, an increase in employee incentive expenses due to improvements in operational performance and a 1.8% increase in average full-time equivalent employees.

Consolidated traffic increased 1.8% and consolidated capacity increased 2.0% during the third quarter of 2016 as compared to the third quarter of 2015. The Company's load factor for the third quarter of 2016 was 85.5%. Consolidated PRASM decreased 5.8% compared to the third quarter of 2015.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Mixed signals with some positive signs of operational performance and demand, but offset by declining pricing power.

factors.summary: Input costs, particularly fuel, are decreasing due to lower prices. The decrease in fuel expenses is significant, with a 27.9% drop in the average price per gallon of aircraft fuel in the first nine months of 2016 and an 18.7% decrease in the third quarter of 2016 compared to the year-ago period.

evidence.text: Aircraft fuel expense decreased $1.6 billion or 27.9%, year-over-year primarily due to a 27.9% decrease in the average price per gallon of aircraft fuel in the first nine months of 2016 compared to the year-ago period. 2016 fuel expense includes the benefit of a $20 million fuel tax refund.

Aircraft fuel expense decreased $331 million, or 17.1%, year-over-year primarily due to a 18.7% decrease in the average price per gallon of aircraft fuel in the third quarter of 2016 compared to the year-ago period.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Significant decrease in fuel prices leading to substantial cost savings.

factors.summary: Management appears confident, as evidenced by significant share repurchases and investments in new products.

evidence.text: In the three and nine months ended September 30, 2016, UAL repurchased 5 million and 48 million shares of UAL common stock in open market transactions, respectively, for $255 million and $2.4 billion, respectively.

In the three and nine months ended September 30, 2016, UAL repurchased 5 million and 48 million shares of its common stock in open market transactions, respectively, for $255 million and $2.4 billion, respectively. The Company plans to introduce new products such as Polaris business class seats.

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: Substantial share repurchases and investments in new products indicate strong confidence in future performance.

factors.summary: Labor costs are increasing, driven by higher pay rates, benefit expenses from new and extended collective bargaining agreements, and increased employee incentive expenses due to improved operational performance. The increase is also supported by a rise in the number of average full-time equivalent employees.

evidence.text: Salaries and related costs increased $418 million or 5.7%, in the first nine months of 2016 as compared to the year-ago period primarily due to higher pay rates and benefit expenses driven by new and extended collective bargaining agreements, an increase in employee incentive expenses due to improvements in operational performance and a 1.8% increase in average full-time equivalent employees.

Salaries and related costs increased $91 million, or 3.6%, in the third quarter of 2016 as compared to the year-ago period primarily due to higher pay rates and benefit expenses driven by new and extended collective bargaining agreements, an increase in employee incentive expenses due to improvements in operational performance and a 3.3% increase in average full-time equivalent employees.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Increases in salaries, benefits, and incentives driven by new agreements and improved performance.

factors.summary: The company is actively investing in fleet efficiency and positioning for future efficiency gains through new aircraft deliveries.

evidence.text: As of September 30, 2016, United had firm commitments and options to purchase aircraft from The Boeing Company (Boeing) and Airbus S.A.S. (Airbus) presented in the table below:
The Company took delivery of four new Boeing 737NG aircraft and one used Airbus A319 aircraft during the third quarter of 2016.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Investment in new aircraft for fleet efficiency and future gains.

factors.summary: Fuel costs are favorable due to lower prices, and the company is effectively hedging a portion of its future fuel requirements, resulting in a positive hedging position and decreased expenses.

evidence.text: Aircraft fuel expense decreased $1.6 billion or 27.9%, year-over-year primarily due to a 27.9% decrease in the average price per gallon of aircraft fuel in the first nine months of 2016 compared to the year-ago period. 2016 fuel expense includes the benefit of a $20 million fuel tax refund.

The average price per gallon of fuel, including fuel taxes, was $1.52 in 2016 compared to $1.87 in 2015. To protect against increases in the prices of aircraft fuel, the Company may hedge a portion of its future fuel requirements.

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: Effective hedging and lower fuel prices leading to decreased expenses.
